records.ety: [F. dtail, fr. dtailler to cut in pieces, tell in detail; pref. d- (L. de or dis-) + tailler to cut. See Tailor.]

defs: A minute portion; one of the small parts; a particular; an item; -- used chiefly in the plural; as, the details of a scheme or transaction.
quotes: The details of the campaign in Italy.

defs: A narrative which relates minute points; an account which dwells on particulars.
The selection for a particular service of a person or a body of men; hence, the person or the body of men so selected.
A minor part, as, in a building, the cornice, caps of the buttresses, capitals of the columns, etc., or (called larger details) a porch, a gable with its windows, a pavilion, or an attached tower.

records.ety: [Cf. F. dtailler to cut up in pieces, tell in detail. See Detail, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Detailed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Detailing.]
usage: Detail respects the act of individualizing the person or body that is separated; detach, the removing for the given end or object.

defs: To relate in particulars; to particularize; to report minutely and distinctly; to enumerate; to specify; as, he detailed all the facts in due order.
To tell off or appoint for a particular service, as an officer, a troop, or a squadron.
To provide with fine or intricate added decoration.
